prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, low-visibility image. Only faint, indistinct shapes are visible, possibly suggesting an outdoor or shadowed scene, but the content cannot be clearly identified from the frame.

At around 4.9 seconds, the image changes to a forward-looking view down a road or path. The road is centered and appears to recede into the distance, bordered by dark trees or foliage on both sides. A bright, glowing area appears ahead at the end of the road, creating strong contrast with the surrounding darkness. The visual impression is of movement or progression toward light, though actual motion cannot be confirmed from the still frame alone.

At around 12 seconds, the image becomes very dark again. There are faint colored areas, including a bluish rectangular shape near the center, but the scene is not clear enough to identify objects or setting with confidence.

The final frame shows a black background with the multicolored Apple logo centered above a line of white text. The visible text reads: “The power to be your best.” The layout is simple and brand-focused, with the rainbow Apple symbol and slogan standing out against the dark background.
